pages.text: AAFT tracks partner distance continuously. If players are in different dimensions, distance is treated as maximum. In practical terms, the system considers the pair critically separated until regroup.

Crossing the effect threshold applies Lontananza to both players. Crossing the damage threshold applies continuous pressure: both players take 1 damage per second until distance returns to safe range.

Threshold scaling is strict and trust-dependent: ticks 1-2 start at effect 50 and damage 75. From tick 3 to tick 8, both thresholds gain +20 blocks per level. Tick 9 jumps to effect 900 and damage 1000.
Practical translation: low trust demands close movement and frequent regroup; high trust allows wider split play, but only with planned timing and active communication.
Bond Compass is your recovery lever when distance control starts collapsing. Use it to close routing mistakes before passive damage turns a navigation issue into a wipe risk.
$(bold)Rule of execution:$() if position awareness is breaking, stop pushing objectives and regroup first. In AAFT, controlled reset is usually stronger than stubborn forward momentum.
